articleTitle: Web-Based Personalized Machine Learning Recommendations to Enhance Shared Decision-Making in Prostate-Specific Antigen Screening: Randomized Controlled Trial.
takeaways: This study examined web-based machine learning tools for prostate cancer screening decisions but only provided the abstract - specific quantitative results about effectiveness, user engagement metrics, or decision quality improvements were not included in the available text

The research focused on men aged 50 and older facing PSA screening decisions, specifically addressing how limited health literacy and cognitive impairments in this population complicate understanding of screening trade-offs between early detection and overdiagnosis risks

The study compared personalized machine learning recommendations against traditional non-personalized decision aids for shared decision-making in prostate cancer screening, though the actual outcomes and measurements are not detailed in the provided abstract
